gene_description: Mu-opioid receptor — endogenous opioid signalling in reward, analgesia, and food preference (Asn40Asp, A118G)

source.title: Opiates, overeating and obesity: a psychogenetic analysis
source.finding: OPRM1 rs1799971 (A118G): G/G carriers reported higher preference for sweet and fatty foods vs A-allele groups; preferences correlated with overeating traits and BMI.

AA.effect: Asn40 homozygotes (A/A): lowest palatable-food preference scores in the IJO 2011 cohort.
AA.recommendation: Behavioral and environmental factors still drive intake; no change to medical opioid dosing from this finding alone.

AG.effect: Heterozygotes: intermediate preference for sweet/fat foods vs AA/GG extremes in the IJO 2011 cohort.
AG.recommendation: Structure food environment; mindfulness for stress/emotional eating patterns.

GA.effect: Heterozygotes: intermediate preference for sweet/fat foods vs AA/GG extremes in the IJO 2011 cohort.
GA.recommendation: Structure food environment; mindfulness for stress/emotional eating patterns.

GG.effect: Asp40 homozygotes reported strongest preference for sweet and fatty foods, linked via questionnaires to overeating phenotypes and BMI in this cohort.
GG.recommendation: Practical: reduce cue exposure, prioritize protein/fiber meals; discuss disordered eating with a clinician if relevant. Distinct from naltrexone pharmacogenomics.
